messages.content:
You are itsy, working in CONTRACT mode. Working directory: /app/personal-site.
Model: unsloth/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-GGUF:IQ2_XXS

YOUR FIRST ACTION MUST BE `propose_contract`. No exceptions, no exploration first.

A contract is the definition of done for the user's task. It is 2–6 short, testable assertions — each one a single thing you can later prove with a shell command:

GOOD:  "the file /app/regex.txt exists"
GOOD:  "running `python3 /tmp/check.py` exits 0"
GOOD:  "`pytest /tests/test_outputs.py -q` reports 3 passed"
BAD:   "the code is correct"          (not testable)
BAD:   "the implementation is complete" (not testable)
BAD:   "all tests pass"              (vague — which tests?)

Until propose_contract returns, NO other tools are available. `write_file`, `patch`, mutating `bash`, etc. will refuse. Read-only tools (read_file, search) are available but you should not need them — you're not exploring, you're stating what 'done' means.

Skip the planning preamble. Skip the analysis. Emit `propose_contract` now with:
- title:       short human title for the task
- brief:       1–2 sentences describing the work
- assertions:  array of {id, text} — pick 2–6

After it returns the toolkit opens up and you can do the work.

Define what 'done' means for the following task by calling propose_contract. Do not start the task yet — just enumerate the checks that would prove it's done.

TASK:
I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master.

function.description: List all indexed projects/repos in the workspace with stats: file count, symbol count, lines of code, languages. Use this FIRST when asked about "the projects", "the codebase", or "what's in this workspace".
function.name: list_projects

function.description: Search the code graph for a symbol, function, or class name. Returns connected code with context. Use for "how does X work" or "find the auth logic" — NOT for listing projects.
function.name: graph_search

query.description: Symbol name or concept to search for

function.description: Get full explanation of a symbol: signature, location, callers, callees, and where it fits in the architecture. Use for "what does X do" questions.
function.name: explain_symbol

function.description: Load relevant project memory for a task. Returns past decisions, workflows, conventions, and gotchas. Call this before starting complex work.
function.name: memory_load

task.description: Task description to find relevant context for

function.description: Read a file. Returns content with line numbers.
function.name: read_file

function.description: Run a shell command. Returns stdout/stderr.
function.name: bash

function.description: Search file contents using regex (ripgrep). Returns matching lines.
function.name: search

function.description: Find files matching a glob pattern.
function.name: find_files

function.description: Search the internet for information. Requires ITSY_WEB_BROWSE=true.
function.name: web_search

function.description: Fetch and extract readable text content from a URL. Requires ITSY_WEB_BROWSE=true.
function.name: web_fetch

function.description: List all stored memory objects. Optionally filter by type.
function.name: memory_list

type.description: Filter by type: decision, workflow, gotcha, convention, context (optional)

function.name: propose_contract

brief.description: 1-3 paragraph plan: what you'll do, the constraints, the shape of done.

features.description: Optional sub-tasks. Each can fulfill one or more assertion IDs.

function.description: Mark a contract assertion `passed` or `failed` with evidence. `passed` requires evidence + at least one verification command (the actual output you saw). Lazy observations like "OK" or "tests passed" are rejected — write what you actually saw.
function.name: mark_assertion

command.description: Command you ran (optional but encouraged for `passed`)

evidence.description: Plain-text explanation of how you verified (≥10 chars)

function.description: Mark a contract feature `in_progress` / `done` / `cancelled`. Optional bookkeeping; not required.
function.name: mark_feature

function.description: Read the current contract state — which assertions are passed / failed / pending.
function.name: contract_status

function.description: Finalize the active contract as `completed`. Refused unless every assertion is `passed`.
function.name: close_contract

function.description: Find files matching a pattern and read the first match. Equivalent to find_files + read_file in one call.
function.name: find_and_read

read_lines.description: Max lines to show from matched file. Default: 50

function.description: Search for a pattern in code, then read the most relevant file found. Equivalent to search + read_file in one call.
function.name: search_and_read

read_context.description: Lines of context around matches. Default: 10

message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to find changes they made on another branch and merge them back into master. Let me first understand the situation by looking at the git history and branches, then propose a contract for finding and merging those changes.

Let me start by proposing a contract that captures what "done" means for this task. The key assertions would be:
1. There exists a branch with the user's previous changes
2. Those changes are found/identified
3. The changes are merged into master
4. The merge is successful (no conflicts, or conflicts resolved)

Let me propose the contract first.

function.arguments: {"assertions":[{"id": "A.001", "text": "A git branch exists containing the user's previous changes (not master)"}, {"id": "A.002", "text": "The specific branch with those changes is identified and documented"}, {"id": "A.003", "text": "The changes are successfully merged into master (git merge completes without conflicts)"}, {"id": "A.004", "text": "After merging, master contains all the files/commits from the previous branch"}],"brief":"Find the branch where the user made their recent changes (likely a stash or a feature branch), identify it, and merge it back into master. If the changes were stashed, restore them; if on another branch, merge that branch into master.","title":"Find lost changes and merge into master"}
function.name: propose_contract
